hero.title: Inspected before it leaves, not just before it's sold.
hero.intro: Quality control happens at every stage of production on our floor, backed by ISO 9001 quality management - not a single check at the end of the line.
hero.imageAlt: Quality inspection during hand-stitching at Kings International

items.title: Hide Grading
items.body: Every hide is graded before it enters the tannery - thickness, grain quality, and blemishes assessed before a single cut is made.

items.title: In-Process Inspection
items.body: Cutting, stitching, and finishing are checked at each stage of the floor, not just at the end - so a defect is caught where it happens, not after the batch is complete.

items.title: Pre-Dispatch Sign-Off
items.body: A final inspection against the agreed specification before anything is packed - stitching density, hardware fit, colour match, and finish are all checked against the approved sample.

items.title: Documentation & Traceability
items.body: Batch records and inspection documentation are kept for every order, so quality issues - if they ever arise - can be traced back to the stage that caused them.

whySection.heading: A sample and a bulk order should be the same product.
whySection.body: Because tanning, cutting, stitching, and finishing all happen in-house, we can hold consistency across a production run in a way that's harder to guarantee when a supply chain is split across multiple subcontractors. What passed inspection in the sample room is what leaves the factory in the container.

cta.heading: Backed by ISO 9001 and seven other standards.
